author_claims.claim_text: Human spinal-cord-derived neural stem cell transplantation has phase I chronic cervical and thoracic safety evidence but remains feasibility-grade rather than efficacy-grade.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Autologous Schwann-cell transplantation has human subacute and chronic safety anchors, but clinical functional efficacy remains unsettled.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: MSC, OEC, BMMC, UCB, and mixed-cell clinical studies mainly support safety and feasibility across small cohorts and heterogeneous routes.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: NeuroRegen scaffold plus cell transplantation is a recurring human clinical strategy, but scaffold, scar resection, and cell effects are inseparable at first pass.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Non-human primate NS/PC transplantation provides an important translational bridge between rodent neural graft experiments and human trials.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Neural grafts can be evaluated for synaptic integration and host-graft connectivity rather than only graft survival or lesion filling.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Circuit-relay claims are strongest when tracing, synaptic mapping, or functional connectivity assays accompany behavioral endpoints.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Human interneuron and organoid approaches extend graft replacement toward regionally specified circuit repair.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Human iPSC-derived spinal progenitors can support sensorimotor recovery signals in immunodeficient or controlled rodent SCI models.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Rehabilitation, synaptic organizers, and growth-factor pretreatment can amplify hiPSC-NS/PC graft effects but complicate attribution to cells alone.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Cell source comparisons indicate that fetal, spinal, brain, ESC, and iPSC-derived NS/PCs should not be treated as one interchangeable product class.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Guidance cues such as BDNF can direct graft-derived axons toward host targets, supporting a directed-connectivity model of repair.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Defined neuronal precursor identity and cryopreservation-thaw platforms are translationally relevant variables for scalable transplantation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: OPC transplantation evidence should be synthesized around remyelination and safety, not assumed to share neural relay mechanisms.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Oligodendrogenic neural progenitors add a chronic cervical repair subtype where glial lineage and compression context matter.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Schwann and glial-support strategies can promote axon growth, remyelination, or connectivity, especially when paired with permissive enzymes or trophic cues.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Restricted precursor grafts often combine with pharmacologic, cycling, bladder, or sensory endpoints, requiring endpoint-specific synthesis.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: OEC transplantation can modulate neuroinflammatory signals such as microglial state or MCP-1 after SCI.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: MSC-family interventions frequently act through scaffold delivery, cell-sheet format, or cytokine-mediated immunomodulation rather than neuronal replacement.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Hydrogel, nanozyme, decellularized matrix, microfiber, exosome, and nanoparticle combinations can strengthen repair signals but limit single-component causal claims.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Chronic scar or lesion microenvironment modification can make grafting more permissive, linking Module 4 evidence back to lesion-architecture findings.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Fetal and embryonic graft studies remain important for circuit and respiratory recovery concepts but carry source, ethics, and translation boundaries.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Autonomic, bladder, pain, respiratory, and cardiovascular outcomes are meaningful transplantation endpoints separate from locomotor recovery.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Clinical feasibility signals should not be promoted to efficacy consensus without controlled endpoints, adverse-event detail, and durability review.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Species context strongly shapes interpretation because rodent, primate, and human SCI transplantation studies test different immunologic and scale constraints.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Immunodeficient host models and xenograft settings support mechanism discovery but require caution for immune-competent translation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Graft survival or differentiation alone is not enough for circuit-restoration claims; connectivity and functional assay alignment matter.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Remyelination, axon support, relay formation, and immunomodulation are different mechanisms that should not be merged into a generic cell-therapy label.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Combination therapies should be tagged as multi-component interventions unless comparator design isolates the cell, scaffold, cargo, or stimulation component.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Human cell-transplant studies span chronic, subacute, acute, complete, and cervical/thoracic contexts, so clinical consensus requires stage- and injury-level stratification.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Activity, rehabilitation, or stimulation can convert grafts into activity-dependent repair platforms rather than passive implants.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Chondroitinase, GDNF, and other permissive modifiers can make Schwann-cell grafts stronger axon-growth platforms but blur cell-only effects.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: OEC evidence spans inflammatory mechanisms and human mucosal/autologous feasibility, but the product class is heterogeneous.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: MSC-family products should be curated as stromal or trophic interventions unless direct neural differentiation is demonstrated.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Cryopreservation and thaw-to-transplant platforms should be captured because logistics can determine whether promising grafts are clinically usable.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Scaffold-cell studies are especially relevant to chronic complete SCI but are also the highest-risk area for over-attribution.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Respiratory and cardiovascular regulation studies show that transplantation may repair specific circuits without broad motor restoration.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Module 4 synthesis should stratify by cell source, lesion stage, species, delivery context, and endpoint before any pooled conclusion.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: Most Module 4B rows are abstract-level first pass, so full-text upgrades are needed before quantitative effect-size consensus.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

author_claims.claim_text: The current corpus supports several consensus-ready qualitative profiles but not a single universal claim that transplantation works after SCI.
author_claims.notes: First-pass author-claim abstraction; full text needed for paper-specific wording.

boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Human phase I-II and pilot rows establish procedural feasibility and safety monitoring more strongly than causal neurological efficacy.

boundary_records.boundary: Graft survival is not circuit repair
boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Survival, differentiation, or lesion filling should not be equated with functional host-graft relay unless connectivity assays support it.
Fetal, spinal, brain, ESC, iPSC, OPC, Schwann, OEC, MSC, BMMC, and UCB products have different intended mechanisms.
Scaffolds, scar resection, growth factors, enzymes, exosomes, hydrogels, stimulation, and rehabilitation can dominate apparent graft effects.

boundary_records.boundary: Species and immune context constrain translation
boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Rodent, immunodeficient, primate, and human contexts should not be pooled without noting immune and scale differences.
Locomotion, sensory pain, bladder, respiratory, cardiovascular, remyelination, histology, and electrophysiology are separate outcome families.
Acute, subacute, chronic, complete, compressive, cervical, thoracic, and scar-resection settings test different biological barriers.

boundary_records.boundary: Abstract-level first pass limits quantitative claims
boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Most 4B rows preserve evidence direction and topic placement; effect sizes, adverse events, and detailed comparator logic require full-text upgrade.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Clinical cell transplantation after SCI is feasible across several small human studies, but the first-pass evidence supports safety monitoring and feasibility more strongly than efficacy.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Clinical cell transplantation after SCI is feasible across several small human studies, but the first-pass evidence supports safety monitoring and feasibility more strongly than efficacy.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Neural progenitor, iPSC, ESC, interneuron, and organoid grafts provide the strongest mechanistic support for host-graft integration when connectivity assays accompany behavioral outcomes.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Neural progenitor, iPSC, ESC, interneuron, and organoid grafts provide the strongest mechanistic support for host-graft integration when connectivity assays accompany behavioral outcomes.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Cell identity and manufacturing variables are core evidence dimensions for Module 4, not metadata afterthoughts.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Cell identity and manufacturing variables are core evidence dimensions for Module 4, not metadata afterthoughts.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Glial, Schwann, OPC, and restricted-precursor strategies form a distinct repair family centered on remyelination, axon support, and endpoint-specific circuitry.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Glial, Schwann, OPC, and restricted-precursor strategies form a distinct repair family centered on remyelination, axon support, and endpoint-specific circuitry.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: OEC, MSC, BMMC, UCB, and stromal-cell interventions should usually be interpreted as trophic, immunomodulatory, or delivery-associated therapies unless direct neuronal replacement is shown.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: OEC, MSC, BMMC, UCB, and stromal-cell interventions should usually be interpreted as trophic, immunomodulatory, or delivery-associated therapies unless direct neuronal replacement is shown.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Scaffold-cell and microenvironment combination therapies are promising for chronic and complete SCI but require cautious attribution because multiple active components are intentionally bundled.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Scaffold-cell and microenvironment combination therapies are promising for chronic and complete SCI but require cautious attribution because multiple active components are intentionally bundled.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Fetal, embryonic, respiratory, autonomic, bladder, pain, and cardiovascular graft studies broaden the transplantation evidence base beyond locomotor recovery.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Fetal, embryonic, respiratory, autonomic, bladder, pain, and cardiovascular graft studies broaden the transplantation evidence base beyond locomotor recovery.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 4 should be synthesized as several mechanism- and context-specific transplantation profiles rather than as one pooled cell-therapy efficacy claim.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 4 should be synthesized as several mechanism- and context-specific transplantation profiles rather than as one pooled cell-therapy efficacy claim.
